items.title: Italy’s Oldest Dairy Taps New Way to Borrow Against Aging Cheese
items.description: Italy’s oldest dairy company has borrowed money against cheese still aging in its warehouse, taking advantage of a change in the law aimed at helping food and wine producers access finance.

items.title: Mubadala Opens Credit Business to Outsiders
items.description: Abu Dhabi's Mubadala Investment Co. is opening up one of its largest investment businesses to outside investors for the first time, Dinesh Nair reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: German Industry Grows More Than Expected on Auto Sector
items.description: German industrial production grew for a second month, another sign that Europe’s largest economy is exiting the drag from the Iran war.

items.title: Temasek-Backed Gym Chain Cult.Fit Files Paperwork for India IPO
items.description: Cult.Fit Ltd. has filed draft paperwork to raise about $100 million in an initial public offering, a rare debut for a gym operator in the world’s most populous country.

items.title: Won’s 24-Hour Trading Debut Passes With Below-Average Volume
items.description: Trading in the Korean won passed with below-average volume in its first day of round-the-clock dealing during a largely uneventful session, compared to the past 30 days.

items.title: Europe’s Defense Stocks Rally Has Much Riding on NATO Summit
items.description: A fledgling rebound in European defense stocks faces a test this week, as investors look to a NATO summit for further clarity on member states’ defense spending plans.
